solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert exact implementation strings like 'strncmp(h->key.data(), "content-length", 14)', 'strncmp(h->value.data(), contentLengthString.data()', 'contentLengthString.data() == nullptr', and 'h->value.length() == 0'. An alternative correct fix using different variable names (e.g., 'clValue' instead of 'contentLengthString'), different comparison methods (e.g., std::string_view::operator==), or different iteration patterns would fail these text-matching tests despite being functionally correc

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The core C++ tests (test_http_parser_validates_duplicate_content_length, test_http_parser_rejects_empty_content_length, test_http_parser_content_length_parsing_preserved) are purely text-matching: they read the source file and assert substrings exist. They never compile the code, run it, or send HTTP requests to verify behavior. The pr-comments.ts test only checks transpilation, not runtime behavior. The only behavioral tests are pass-to-pass repo tests (lint, ban-words, bun-types, smuggling tes

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub pr-comments.ts that is simply an empty valid TypeScript file (e.g., 'export {}') would pass test_pr_comments_script_parses. For the C++ tests, since they're text-matching, an agent could just append a comment block containing all the required strings without implementing any logic, and the tests would pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires the script to 'accept PR number, URL, or current branch as input', 'support --json flag', fetch three GitHub endpoints, and present chronological listings. None of this runtime behavior is tested — only transpilation is checked. The instruction also requires identical duplicates to be accepted, but no test verifies this acceptance behavior.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with clear section comments (fail-to-pass, pass-to-pass static, pass-to-pass repo_tests), each function has a descriptive name and docstring explaining what it checks.

structured_data_schema.reason: The instruction documents the JSON schema (when, user, kind, location, body, url, resolved, outdated) but no test validates that the script actually produces output conforming to this schema. The only test for pr-comments.ts checks transpilation, not output format.

no_typos.reason: File paths, variable names, commands, and literal strings in instruction.md and tests are consistent and free of typos.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md specifies exact implementation details: the precise loop pattern ('for (HttpRequest::Header *h = req->headers; (++h)->key.length(); )'), exact variable names ('contentLengthString'), exact comparison expressions, and exact error return value. This goes far beyond naming the file or describing the bug — it provides the complete code solution.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold config diffs (package.json, CLAUDE.md) are localized additions — a single line in package.json and an appended section in CLAUDE.md — and don't contradict other files.
